typecheck.command: ROOT=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && cd "$ROOT/erp-domain" && MIGRATION_SET=v2 mvn -T8 compile -q

lint.command: ROOT=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && cd "$ROOT" && bash ci/lint-knowledgebase.sh && bash ci/check-architecture.sh && bash ci/check-enterprise-policy.sh && bash ci/check-orchestrator-layer.sh && python3 scripts/check_flaky_tags.py --tests-root erp-domain/src/test/java --gate gate-fast && bash scripts/guard_openapi_contract_drift.sh

blockingIssues.description: SecurityMonitoringService now triggers a notification email on every post-threshold suspicious event because `increaseSuspiciousScore()` uses `>= suspiciousActivityThreshold` and `handleSuspiciousActivity()` always notifies. PasswordResetService routes repeated reset abuse through that path, so one noisy identifier can generate unbounded alert-email spam once `security.monitoring.notification-email` is configured.

DashboardWindow now parses `<digits>d` windows with `Integer.parseInt(daysPart)` and no overflow handling. Oversized user-controlled values such as `999999999999d` can throw `NumberFormatException`, and EnterpriseDashboardService forwards the raw `window` query parameter directly, so malformed dashboard requests can now produce HTTP 500s.

SalesController no longer binds `X-Idempotency-Key`, but SalesCoreEngine still proceeds when the canonical idempotency key resolves to null. Legacy callers retrying with only the deprecated header now lose idempotency instead of failing closed with a 4xx, creating a duplicate-order risk on `POST /api/v1/sales/orders`.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require script/guard cleanup handoffs to state the chosen safety mode and why, so scrutiny can verify whether the worker followed the intended guard-first or characterization-first procedure without reverse-engineering the transcript.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `foundation-broken-script-refs` review found that `.factory/skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md` expects the handoff to capture the selected safety mode, but the worker handoff omitted it even though the packet was primarily a script/guard cleanup.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify that retiring legacy idempotency headers must fail closed at runtime (filter/interceptor or equivalent), not merely stop binding the old header in controller method signatures.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `foundation-deprecated-forremoval-cleanup` review found that removing `X-Idempotency-Key` binding from `SalesController` left sales-order creation fail-open because `SalesCoreEngine` still accepts a null canonical key. Current mission/library wording about rejecting stale headers was not concrete enough to prevent that implementation.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.target: module guidance / internal frontend handoff docs
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Align payroll-boundary guidance and internal frontend handoff references with the post-cleanup canonical payroll path so future workers and consumers stop following the retired batch payroll endpoint and DTOs.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `foundation-payroll-dead-code-removal` review passed the code changes, but it found mission/module AGENTS guidance and `.factory/library/frontend-handoff.md` still reference the removed batch payroll surface while runtime now preserves only the canonical single-payment path.

rejectedObservations.observation: Document the scoped Spotless fallback / formatter-only packet handling in shared worker guidance.
rejectedObservations.reason: duplicate: prior scrutiny syntheses already captured the same recommendation (for example `.factory/validation/accounting-core/scrutiny/synthesis.round5.json` and `.factory/validation/support-host-split/scrutiny/synthesis.json`), so this round adds no distinct new remediation beyond repeating the gap.

rejectedObservations.observation: Add `.factory/services.yaml` commands for scoped Spotless, `-Xlint:all` compile, Flyway guard contract, and narrow sales+HR proof lanes.
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous: the reports identify convenience gaps, but they do not establish a single stable command shape worth codifying during a failing validation round, and the existing manifest plus direct script invocations were sufficient to complete scrutiny.

rejectedObservations.observation: Document `security.monitoring.notification-email` in shared library guidance immediately.
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous: the only feature introducing that property failed scrutiny due to alert-spam behavior, so documenting the knob before the runtime semantics are corrected would risk freezing incorrect guidance.

rejectedObservations.observation: Update shared guidance to advertise benchmark-admin runtime bootstrap and the `erp-domain` working-directory quirk.
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented-or-low-value: the benchmark-admin note is feature-local docs drift rather than a systemic worker-procedure issue, and the working-directory requirement is already encoded in the shared `.factory/services.yaml` commands used by workers.
